_metadata.description: Reference ranges for 15 core biomarkers, derived from Indian population studies and ICMR/WHO guidelines. Lab's own printed range always takes precedence in the application. These values serve as fallback when lab range extraction fails.
_metadata.note: Range priority in application: (1) Lab printed range, (2) This ICMR/Indian-study-derived range, (3) Flag to user if both unavailable.

HGB.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Eat iron-rich Indian foods: palak (spinach), methi, rajma, chana, and jaggery daily. Pair with Vitamin C (lemon juice) to improve absorption.
HGB.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: पालक, मेथी, राजमा, चना और गुड़ खाएं। विटामिन C के लिए नींबू का रस डालें।
HGB.source: WHO Haemoglobin cutoffs for anaemia diagnosis (WHO/NHD/MNH/11.1, 2011). Anemia thresholds: Males <13.0, Females <12.0, Pregnant <11.0. Upper normal from Apollo Hospitals multicentric Indian reference interval study (PMC4062657, n=10,665 healthy Indians across 4 cities).

HBA1C.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Reduce rice and maida intake. Replace with whole wheat roti, daliya (broken wheat), and add bitter gourd (karela) and fenugreek seeds (methi dana) to your meals.
HBA1C.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: चावल और मैदा कम करें। गेहूं की रोटी, दलिया खाएं, और करेला-मेथी दाना शामिल करें।
HBA1C.source: American Diabetes Association Standards of Medical Care 2024, Section 2 — adopted by RSSDI (Research Society for Study of Diabetes in India) Clinical Practice Recommendations 2022. ICMR-INDIAB study used HbA1c ≥6.5% as diabetes diagnostic threshold (PMC3192597). Prediabetes: 5.7–6.4%.

GLUCOSE_F.urgency_if_critical_low: Go to hospital immediately — severe hypoglycemia
GLUCOSE_F.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Reduce rice portions. Eat more vegetables like lauki (bottle gourd), karela (bitter gourd). Choose whole wheat over maida. Include moong dal and palak.
GLUCOSE_F.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: चावल कम खाएं। लौकी, करेला, मूंग दाल और पालक खाएं। मैदे की जगह गेहूं उपयोग करें।
GLUCOSE_F.source: ICMR-INDIAB study diagnostic criteria (PMC3192597). ADA 2024 (adopted by RSSDI 2022): Normal FPG 70–99 mg/dL, IFG 100–125 mg/dL, Diabetes ≥126 mg/dL. Critical low <40 mg/dL (CLSI panic threshold). Critical high >450 mg/dL per labpedia.net clinical reference.

TSH.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Avoid raw cruciferous vegetables like cabbage and cauliflower in large amounts. Include iodine-rich foods like iodised salt. Selenium-rich foods like sunflower seeds may help.
TSH.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: बंद गोभी और फूल गोभी कच्ची कम खाएं। आयोडीन युक्त नमक उपयोग करें।
TSH.source: Indian Thyroid Society (ITS) Guidelines for Management of Hypothyroidism 2019 — recommends TSH normal range 0.4–4.0 mIU/L for non-pregnant adults. Tighter pregnancy-specific ranges per ITS. Reference range 0.35–4.94 mIU/L used in Indian CKD study (ijcbr.in). Critical values from CLSI/clinical practice.

CHOL_TOTAL.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Reduce ghee, butter, red meat. Use mustard or groundnut oil in moderation. Eat more oats, isabgol (psyllium husk), and fresh vegetables daily.
CHOL_TOTAL.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: घी, मक्खन और लाल मांस कम करें। सरसों या मूंगफली का तेल उपयोग करें। जई, इसबगोल और सब्जियां खाएं।
CHOL_TOTAL.source: Cardiological Society of India (CSI) / ICMR-NPCDCS Guidelines: Desirable <200 mg/dL, Borderline 200–239, High ≥240. Consistent with ATP III / AHA classification adopted for Indian practice. Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657) reported reference interval up to 254 mg/dL in healthy Indians, reflecting higher baseline, but clinical decision thresholds follow CSI guidelines.

LDL.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Avoid vanaspati, palm oil, full-fat dairy. Use olive oil or cold-pressed mustard oil. Garlic and onion daily help reduce LDL. Eat more dals and legumes.
LDL.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: वनस्पति और पाम तेल बंद करें। कच्ची घानी सरसों या जैतून तेल उपयोग करें। लहसुन-प्याज रोज खाएं।
LDL.source: ATP III / AHA LDL classification adopted by Cardiological Society of India. Labpedia clinical reference shows >160 mg/dL as actionable high. Apollo Indian study (PMC4062657) reported observed 95% interval 60–176 mg/dL in healthy Indians.

HDL.note: For HDL, LOW value is the problem. A low HDL is a cardiac risk factor.
HDL.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Increase physical activity. Eat more omega-3 rich foods: flaxseeds (alsi), walnuts, fish. Avoid trans fats and vanaspati. A daily walk of 30 minutes raises HDL.
HDL.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: अलसी, अखरोट, मछली खाएं। ट्रांस फैट बंद करें। रोज 30 मिनट टहलें।
HDL.source: AHA/ATP III HDL thresholds: <40 mg/dL (males) is cardiac risk factor, <50 mg/dL (females). These thresholds adopted in Indian clinical practice per Cardiological Society of India. Labpedia clinical reference: critical HDL <40 mg/dL = cardiac risk. Apollo Indian study (PMC4062657) used same thresholds.

TRIGLYCERIDES.note_on_indian_range: Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657) showed observed 95th percentile: Males 55–267 mg/dL, Females 52–207 mg/dL — notably higher than Western reference due to carbohydrate-heavy Indian diet. Clinical decision thresholds still follow ATP III.
TRIGLYCERIDES.urgency_if_critical_high: within 48 hours — very high triglycerides risk acute pancreatitis
TRIGLYCERIDES.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Significantly reduce rice, sugar, maida, and alcohol. Eat fewer sweets and fried snacks. Add flaxseeds (alsi) and fish to your diet. Evening walks help lower triglycerides.
TRIGLYCERIDES.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: चावल, चीनी, मैदा बहुत कम करें। मिठाई और तले हुए नाश्ते बंद करें। अलसी और मछली खाएं।
TRIGLYCERIDES.source: ATP III classification: Normal <150, Borderline 150–199, High 200–499, Very High ≥500 mg/dL. Adopted by CSI. Indian population-specific observed ranges from PMC4062657 (Apollo multicentric study). Critical high ≥1000 mg/dL: acute pancreatitis risk, clinical emergency.

CREATININE.note_on_indian_range: Indian males tend toward lower muscle mass than Caucasians; creatinine upper limit is 1.4 mg/dL per Indian clinical biochemistry studies (ijcbr.in study). Lower than some Western labs that use 1.5.
CREATININE.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24 hours — possible acute kidney injury
CREATININE.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Reduce high-protein foods: dal, paneer, meat. Drink 8–10 glasses of water daily. Avoid excessive salt.
CREATININE.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: दाल, पनीर, मांस कम करें। रोज 8-10 गिलास पानी पिएं। अधिक नमक से बचें।
CREATININE.source: Indian clinical biochemistry study (ijcbr.in): Serum Creatinine reference range 0.7–1.4 mg/dL (males), 0.6–1.2 mg/dL (females) for Indian population. Supported by SKIMS Kashmir study (Acta Scientific, 2024, n=3181 Indian subjects). Critical high >10 mg/dL per CLSI/labpedia.net panic values.

UREA.note: Indian labs report Blood Urea (not BUN). BUN = Urea × 0.467. Reference range 13–45 mg/dL used across Indian clinical labs (ijcbr.in study). Some labs extend to 50 mg/dL.
UREA.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Reduce non-veg protein: chicken, mutton, fish. Limit dal intake temporarily. Drink adequate water.
UREA.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: मांसाहारी खाना और दाल कम करें। पर्याप्त पानी पिएं।
UREA.source: Reference range 13–45 mg/dL from Indian clinical biochemistry practice (ijcbr.in: Int J Clin Biochem Res). SKIMS Kashmir observational study (n=3181): median serum urea 41 mg/dL in Indian subjects. Critical high >100 mg/dL per labpedia.net/CLSI panic value (uremia risk).

ALT.note_on_indian_range: Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657) showed higher ALT upper limits in Delhi center versus South Indian center — regional variation exists. 56 U/L used as conservative upper limit for males.
ALT.urgency_if_critical_high: within 24 hours — possible acute liver injury
ALT.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Avoid alcohol completely. Reduce oily and fried food. Eat moong dal khichdi, daliya. Drink turmeric milk (haldi doodh) — curcumin supports liver function.
ALT.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: शराब बिल्कुल बंद करें। तला हुआ खाना कम करें। मूंग दाल खिचड़ी और हल्दी वाला दूध पिएं।
ALT.source: Indian population reference range: Males 7–56 U/L, Females 7–45 U/L. Consistent with Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657). Springer Indian J Clinical Biochemistry (Arora et al.) used same ALT ranges for Indian subjects. Critical high >500 U/L = severe hepatocellular injury (CLSI).

AST.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Same advice as ALT — avoid alcohol, fried foods. Amla (Indian gooseberry) and turmeric are traditional liver-protective foods.
AST.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: शराब और तला हुआ बंद करें। आंवला और हल्दी लिवर के लिए फायदेमंद हैं।
AST.source: Standard clinical reference range used across Indian hospital biochemistry labs. Validated in Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657). Springer Indian J Clinical Biochemistry (Arora et al., 2009) used identical ranges for Indian subjects. Critical high >500 U/L per CLSI guidance.

URIC_ACID.note_on_indian_range: Assam Indian population study (PMC3942193, n=1470): observed reference range 2.6–8.2 mg/dL overall. Apollo Indian study (PMC4062657) showed male uric acid reference interval 3.5–8.2 mg/dL — slightly higher upper limit than Western labs. Gout threshold: >6.8 mg/dL.
URIC_ACID.urgency_if_critical_high: within 48 hours — risk of kidney stones and gout attack
URIC_ACID.indian_diet_tip_high_en: Avoid red meat, organ meats, and dals like rajma, masoor. Limit alcohol. Drink lots of water. Cherries (if available) help lower uric acid.
URIC_ACID.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: लाल मांस, राजमा, मसूर कम करें। शराब बंद करें। खूब पानी पिएं।
URIC_ACID.source: PMC3942193 (Assam, India, n=1470 healthy adults): reference range 2.6–8.2 mg/dL. Apollo multicentric (PMC4062657): males 3.5–8.2 mg/dL. Standard clinical cut-offs: Males >7.2, Females >6.0 = hyperuricemia (labpedia.net). Critical high >12 mg/dL = acute urate nephropathy risk (CLSI).

PLATELETS.unit_note: Indian labs often report in Lakhs/cumm. 1 Lakh = 100,000. Convert: multiply Lakhs value by 100,000.
PLATELETS.note_on_india: Dengue fever is endemic in India and causes platelet crash below 20,000. Any count <50,000 requires urgent attention. Apollo Indian study (PMC4062657): Males 1.3–3.8 Lakhs, Females 1.3–4.2 Lakhs per µL.
PLATELETS.urgency_if_emergency_low: Go to hospital today — severe bleeding risk (possible dengue or other cause)
PLATELETS.indian_diet_tip_low_en: Eat papaya leaves juice (papita patta) — traditional remedy shown to help in dengue-related thrombocytopenia. Eat pomegranate and gourd. Rest well, avoid aspirin.
PLATELETS.indian_diet_tip_low_hi: पपीते के पत्तों का रस पिएं। अनार और लौकी खाएं। एस्पिरिन बिल्कुल न लें।
PLATELETS.source: Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657): Males 1.3–3.8 × 10⁵/µL, Females 1.3–4.2 × 10⁵/µL in 10,665 healthy Indians. Critical low <50,000/µL per labpedia.net CLSI panic values. Emergency threshold <20,000/µL is common Indian lab panic value due to dengue prevalence. Critical high >1,000,000/µL (labpedia.net).

WBC.note_on_indian_range: Indian labs commonly use 4,000–10,000/µL or 4,000–11,000/µL. Apollo Indian study and South Indian ethnic variation study (PubMed 18206601) showed lower upper WBC limits in Indian population vs Western reference. 4,000–10,000/µL used as conservative Indian standard.
WBC.indian_diet_tip_high_en: High WBC often signals infection. Rest, stay hydrated with nimbu pani and coconut water. See a doctor — do not self-medicate with antibiotics.
WBC.indian_diet_tip_high_hi: उच्च WBC संक्रमण का संकेत है। नींबू पानी और नारियल पानी पिएं। खुद एंटीबायोटिक न लें।
WBC.source: Indian labs standard range: 4,000–10,000/µL. Supported by Apollo multicentric Indian study (PMC4062657) and South Indian ethnic variation study (PubMed PMID 18206601) showing lower upper WBC limits in Indian vs Western populations. Critical low <2,000 = severe immunosuppression (CLSI). Critical high >30,000 = leukocytosis warranting urgent evaluation (CLSI/labpedia.net).
